data.content: Now I'll create the `.template.config/template.json` file to turn this into a reusable `dotnet new` template.
data.reasoningText: Now I need to create a .NET template from this project. A .NET template requires a `.template.config` directory with a `template.json` file. I should also make the namespace and project name configurable via the `sourceName` parameter so when someone creates a new project from this template, the name gets replaced.

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-5b6c4ebea92b459e9df4f7c0d0d73fd9/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json b/tmp/sv-5b6c4ebea92b459e9df4f7c0d0d73fd9/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-5b6c4ebea92b459e9df4f7c0d0d73fd9/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+{
+  "$schema": "http://json.schemastore.org/template",
+  "author": "Team",
+  "classifications": ["Test", "xUnit"],
+  "identity": "TeamTestProject",
+  "name": "Team Test Project",
+  "shortName": "team-test",
+  "tags": {
+    "language": "C#",
+    "type": "project"
+  },
+  "sourceName": "MyTests",
+  "preferNameDirectory": true
+}
+

The template "Team Test Project" was created successfully.

=== .csproj ===
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net9.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2">
      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
    </PackageReference>
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
=== UnitTest1.cs ===
using Xunit;

namespace SampleTests;
public class UnitTest1
{
    [Fact]
    public void Test1() { }
}

data.content:
Template is working. The `.csproj` got named `SampleTests.csproj` and the namespace in `UnitTest1.cs` was replaced with `SampleTests`.

**What was created:** `.template.config/template.json` inside `test-output/MyTests/`.

**How your team uses it:**

1. **Install:** `dotnet new install ./test-output/MyTests`
2. **Create a project:** `dotnet new team-test -n MyNewTests`

The `sourceName` setting automatically replaces `MyTests` with the provided `-n` name in filenames, namespaces, and the `.csproj`.
